market_regime: Tech-led risk-off with energy exceptionalism; VIX 18.92 elevated, XLK -5.9% 5d, XLE +1.8%, rotation to defensives/commodities; Hormuz crisis dominates macro

scanner_blind_spots.reason: Scanner missed the single most asymmetric near-term binary catalyst in the universe. VIKTORIA-1 hit primary endpoints in BOTH Phase 3 cohorts (announced ASCO June 2). PDUFA July 17 under RTOR. 29.4% SI creates squeeze on approval. Scanner's catalyst detection apparently didn't trigger on this despite being in public FDA calendar data.

Scanner has LNG and E&P energy names but missed the direct tanker operators who are the most operationally levered beneficiaries of the Hormuz crisis. Suezmax rates at $121,800/day vs $14,900 fleet breakeven = extraordinary earnings not yet visible in screener-based metrics.

Same Hormuz tanker blind spot as TNK — International Seaways fleet-wide TCE >$100,000/day and just declared $4.55/share special dividend. Scanner's energy screen focused on upstream/midstream and missed the shipping operators.

Scanner found NVDA (the GPU designer) but missed Vertiv (the critical power/cooling infrastructure that NVDA GPUs physically require). $15B backlog, 252% liquid cooling order growth, PEG below 1 at 51% EPS growth — this is the most direct AI infrastructure dependency the scanner didn't flag.

Scanner has various energy names but missed this clean value + LNG step-change story. Port Arthur LNG first production 2027 is an irreversible FCF upgrade that doesn't show up in trailing metrics. Fwd P/E 12.9 / $5.29B FCF / Zacks #1 in energy rotation.

Scanner likely missed BioNTech because trailing COVID revenue is declining. However: $1B buyback commenced June 8 at a company trading near its $4.5B cash value — the oncology pipeline (25+ Phase 2/3 trials) comes essentially free. Druckenmiller backing adds smart-money signal.

The only US company with both uranium processing scale AND heavy rare earth production — a dual critical minerals play the scanner's sector-specific energy/mining screens cannot easily cross-reference. ASM acquisition (June 2026) creates the first full mine-to-metal western REE supply chain.
